sections.title: What's New
items: Offline Recipe Browser: Recipes you've browsed before now load instantly — even when you're offline or on a flaky connection. No more empty lists. A manual refresh button lets you pull the latest from the cloud whenever you're ready.

Recipe Re-run: Re-run any previous recipe execution directly from the run info modal. Your original parameters are pre-filled and editable, so you can tweak inputs and re-run with a single click — no need to set everything up from scratch.
Recipe Run History: Every recipe execution is now tracked with its parameters, status, and timing — giving you a clear record of what ran, when, and with what inputs.

Recipe Run Deletion: Deleting a recipe run now does a thorough cleanup — all generated sheets, charts, and dashboards from that run are removed, and any charts pinned to your own dashboards are cleaned up gracefully instead of leaving broken placeholders.

sections.title: Fixes
items: Fixed internal data-handling issues that could cause recipe run timestamps to display incorrectly or recipe deletions to silently fail.
Older recipes that were missing certain identifiers no longer produce duplicate outputs when re-run.

sections.title: What's New
items: Project Settings: A new project-level settings panel lets you view project details, customize data appearance (header and row background colors), configure formatting for numbers, dates, and timezones, and delete projects — all in one place.
Dashboard Enhancements: Edit chart titles and quickly remove charts directly from the dashboard. Also fixed CSS issues that broke the UI when building dashboards with a large number of charts.

sections.title: What's New
items: Redesigned Onboarding: The onboarding experience has been completely revamped with clearer messaging, better guidelines, and smoother navigation to help new users get up and running faster.
Smarter AI Visualizations: AI-powered visualizations now have more context to work with, resulting in noticeably better performance and more relevant chart output.
View/Edit Recipe: You can now view and edit recipes — a first step towards the full recipe framework, giving you more visibility and control over your data transformation steps.

sections.title: Fixes
items: S3 Multipart Download Fix: S3 multipart downloads could fail silently and leave corrupted files on disk. Downloads now complete reliably.

sections.title: What's New
items: Folder Sync & Multi-File Merge: Point OrcaSheets at an entire directory of data files (CSV, Excel, Parquet, TSV) and it automatically merges sheets that share the same schema into one unified sheet. Different schemas stay separate. Use the new Sync Project option to re-sync when files change or new ones appear.
Keyboard Shortcuts: New keyboard shortcuts for faster navigation — switching between tabs, opening files, and getting around the app. See Help → Keyboard Shortcuts for the full list.
Smarter AI Responses: OrcAI and AI-powered report generation produce more useful, accurate output.

sections.title: Fixes
items: Better Pivot Query Handling: Query responses for PIVOT operations tightened up for more reliable results.
AWS Glue Connector - Hourly Partition Fix: Hourly partitioned tables now sync incrementally as expected.

sections.title: What's New
items: OrcaSheets Connector: A new native connector that pulls your OrcaSheets cloud data straight into the local app over Iceberg/Parquet, authenticated with JWT API credentials. Includes incremental sync out of the box, auto-detecting watermarks from existing local Parquet files.
More Robust Chart Support: Charts handle more edge cases without falling over.

sections.title: Fixes
items: AWS Glue Connector - Incremental Column Fix: Epoch timestamp columns are now compared as proper datetimes instead of raw numbers, fixing incremental syncs across MSSQL, ClickHouse, and Snowflake. Incremental syncs with zero new rows now skip cleanly instead of throwing an error.

sections.title: What's New
items: Multi-Sheet Chat for Everyone: AI Chat now reads your whole workbook by default, not just the active sheet. Too many sheets? Hand-pick the ones that matter. Previously enterprise-only.
Faster Report Generation: Fixed unnecessary full HTML rebuilds on every report generation, improving load times.
Smarter Connector Re-sync: Re-sync now covers derived sheets alongside originals, and connector stats are back where they belong.

sections.title: Fixes
items: CSV Duplicate Columns: Fixed an error that occurred when importing CSV files with duplicate column names.
Excel Duplicate Row: Resolved an issue where importing any Excel file would generate an extra duplicate row.
SQL Connector Table Config: Fixed an issue where table configuration was being ignored for SQL connectors when uploading JSON config.

sections.title: Improvements
items: Faster OrcAI Responses: Improved performance of OrcAI with added retry logic for more reliable results, even under intermittent failures.
Database Connector Views: Added support for views across database connectors, so you can now query and work with views alongside tables.
Resizable Window: The application window is now freely resizable, giving you more control over your workspace layout.

sections.title: Fixes
items: Postgres Connector Fix: Resolved a regression in the Postgres connector caused by sample connection configs interfering with active connections.

sections.title: Improvements
items: More Resilient CSV Import: CSV parsing now attempts a strict read first, then progressively falls back to less strict parsing when needed — improving compatibility with messy or inconsistent CSVs.

sections.title: Fixes
items: Regression Fix: Restored support for creating an empty project, which could fail in certain cases after recent changes.

sections.title: What's New
items: Snowflake Connector (+ Sample Configs): Connect directly to Snowflake from the connectors list, with sample connection configs so you can quickly test connections without starting from a blank form.

AI SQL Editor (Beta): Describe what you need in plain English—merging sheets, cleaning messy data, or building filtered views from raw sources—and we generate the SQL and results into a fresh, refreshable sheet. This is a premium feature; message us to get it enabled.

Single Window Architecture: Consolidated the splash screen, onboarding, and main interface into a single full-screen window to align with macOS 26 changes, reducing crashes and simplifying the experience.
Recent Activity Tracking: Projects now sort by what you actually worked on last, and screens remember the tab you were on so it’s easier to pick up where you left off.

sections.title: Fixes
items: Editing messages in the chat container works again after a regression in the last release.
Downloading connector configs no longer exposes database passwords in plain text; sensitive fields are now obfuscated in the exported JSON.

sections.title: Stability Improvements
items: Fixed startup crashes: Resolved an issue where OrcaSheets could crash immediately on launch for some users. OrcaSheets now opens reliably.

sections.title: What's New
items: SQL Connector Rebuilt — Two-step process: plug in credentials, pick your tables, then connect. You can test the connection before committing.
Merge Data — Combine multiple sheets with the same schema into one from Update Data in the grid toolbar. Delete source sheets after if you like.
Scratch Projects — Create an empty project without uploading a dummy CSV; pull data straight from your connectors.
Dashboard Upgrades — Create a new dashboard directly from the "Add to Dashboard" flow. Adding a visualisation no longer navigates you away from what you were working on.

sections.title: Fixes
items: Sankey charts no longer crash on cyclic data (cycles are filtered out and downloadable).
Copy/Paste commands work reliably again across the application.

sections.title: Improvements & Fixes
items: Delete data files directly and bulk delete multiple sheets for easier workspace management.
Window menu bar options fixed for a more reliable desktop experience.
Auto-updater popup redesigned for clearer, less disruptive update prompts.
Downloaded files now keep the correct file names.
Project paths handled correctly on Windows (correct path separators).

sections.title: New: Open Your Data Files Instantly
items: Make OrcaSheets your default app for CSV, XLSX, and Parquet files — double-click and start analysing immediately.
Open data files with a double-click; no more manual imports.
Launch files directly from downloads, email attachments, or shared folders for faster workflow.
Consistent OrcaSheets experience across your system whenever you open a data file.

sections.title: What's New
items: Bug fix for CSV import with "Time" column type.
Styling fixes for published chart and dashboard.
Based on your feedback! Thanks for helping us improve.
Release version 26.1.2

sections.title: What's New
items: Seamless Onboarding — No more token copy-paste required. Get started instantly.
Auto Updater — Updates happen automatically; no more manual "Download the latest version" messages.
Enjoy the smoother experience!

sections.title: New Visualizations
items: Stacked Chart — Compare component contributions within a total over time or across categories.
Chart From Chat — Generate a chart from a natural-language prompt (cloud-backed for speed and scalability).

sections.title: Interaction & Analysis
items: Column-Level Sorting & Multi-Sort — Sort any column with multiple sort priorities.
Summarization — One-click automated summary of key trends and patterns for the current data view.

sections.title: Filtering & Views
items: Save Filters — Persist filter configurations and reapply them instantly.
Auto Filter Naming — Saved filters get sensible default names.
Keyboard Navigation for Filter Panel — Navigate and apply filters without a mouse.

sections.title: Reliability
items: Soft-Fail Widgets — Failed widgets show a non-blocking error with Retry; other content keeps loading.

sections.title: Design & Accessibility
items: Harmonized Color Palette — Updated palette for readability, contrast, and accessibility.
Keyboard Navigation for Dashboard Widgets — Move focus between widgets and use actions via keyboard.

sections.title: Infrastructure
items: Prompt Processing Moved to Cloud — Natural-language features (chart from chat, summarization) run in the cloud for faster responses and scalability.
